findings.summary: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 Step 1: PROP-016 Mech A IS wired into monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md (Step 2b, lines 161-223). Pattern is `^(test\.js|build\.js|CLAUDE\.md|build-scripts/|monitor/prompts/)`. It HALTs commit and writes a stranded-patches sentinel when any NEVER_PUSH file appears in `git status --porcelain` output before commit.

evidence.key_lines: L177 (NEVER_PUSH_HITS extraction), L178 (gate), L181 (HALT log), L194 (rationale), L221 (PATCHES_STRANDED=1 set)

findings.summary: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 Step 2: The 2026-05-18 false closures (ISS-2094, ISS-2102, ISS-2106) slipped through Mech A because they were a DIFFERENT failure class than Mech A is designed to catch.
findings.diagnosis: Mech A checks `git status --porcelain` for modifications to NEVER_PUSH files BEFORE commit. The 2026-05-18 closures never modified any file — the decider LLM hallucinated fix_descriptions, wrote status='fixed' on closed-issues entries, and committed/pushed ONLY the close-issues update. From git's perspective, no NEVER_PUSH file was modified, so Mech A's pattern match was empty, no NEVER_PUSH_HITS, no stranded-patches sentinel. Mech A is functionally correct for its design scope (catching unintended NEVER_PUSH-file commits in mixed-data commits); it just isn't a defense against pure-hallucination closures because there's no file modification to detect.
findings.tell_in_iss_2106: fix_description literally reads 'updated build-scripts/generate-html.js line 500 from 69 dome-claimed wins to 69 dome-claimed wins, 1 analyst-added claim (WIN-070)' — the from-text and to-text both contain '69 dome-claimed wins'. The LLM pasted the same substring into both sides of its own fix description, exposing that no actual read-and-write was performed.

findings.summary: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 Step 3: PROP-058 authored (monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-058-mandatory-pending-verification-on-self-apply-closures.json) to close the gap.
findings.design_summary: Promote `fixed-pending-verification` + `verification_pattern` from 'recommended/optional optimization' to MANDATORY for every decider-self-apply* close. Add immediate pre-push self-check (run verification_pattern against working tree; if fails, treat as hallucination and don't push close). Add integrity-agent daily audit that scans closed-issues.json for status='fixed' + decider close + null verification_pattern (Mech-A-bypass candidates).
findings.counter_considerations_noted: PROP body includes explicit alternatives_considered and counter_considerations sections (per operator preference 'Think about the other side of the argument'): friction added per close (~1-2KB context, acceptable), fingerprint extraction edge cases (deletion-only patches use inverse pattern), what if decider hallucinates the fingerprint too (defense-in-depth via change_3 immediate self-check), legitimate fingerprintless closes (none — all legitimate closes have a fingerprint, even if it's a JSON flag value or an inverse-presence pattern).

findings.summary: DIRECTIVE-20260525-001 Steps 4-5: 7-day audit (2026-05-18 through 2026-05-25) found 11 decider-self-apply* close candidates targeting NEVER_PUSH file types (build-scripts/*, build.js, test.js, CLAUDE.md, monitor/prompts/*, docs/*). All 11 verified against git origin/main. 10 are LEGITIMATE fixes (the substantive change is present in origin). 1 (ISS-2106) is one of the 3 known false closures from the directive's evidence list; its underlying problem was fixed by commit 0b9f6c0 (via separate ISS-2112), but the ISS-2106 closed-issues entry still lacks a false_closure_audit annotation. No NEW false closures discovered.

candidate_with_audit_gap.current_status: closed-issues entry shows status='fixed', fixed_by='decider-2026-05-18T06-10Z', fix_description is the hallucinated 'from 69 to 69' text
candidate_with_audit_gap.underlying_problem_state: FIXED — build-scripts/generate-html.js line 500 currently reads '70 dome-claimed wins plus 2 numbered-collision sub-claims' per origin/main
candidate_with_audit_gap.audit_trail_gap: no false_closure_audit annotation block (unlike ISS-2094 and ISS-2102 which DO have such blocks)
candidate_with_audit_gap.operator_action: OPTIONAL — annotate the closed-issues.json entry to match the ISS-2094/ISS-2102 pattern. Not blocking; the substantive problem is gone. Listed here for record-keeping completeness.
